title: SubAuth
description: Automatic whitelisting for your Twitch subscribers!
body:
## SubAuth

SubAuth is a server-sided mod or plugin, designed to be used by content creators on Twitch who wish to create servers for their subscribers to play on. SubAuth automatically whitelists your subscribers who have linked their Twitch accounts to SubAuth via the SubAuth server.

## Setup for Server Admins

1. Log in to `subauth.thatgamerblue.com` using your Minecraft client, with version 1.15 or newer.
2. Link your Twitch account following the steps in the chatbox. Make sure to get your token, you'll need it in a minute.
3. Install the SubAuth mod or plugin on your server. After first run, it will create a config file for you to put in your tokens.
4. Put your token in the config file, either in `config/subauth.json` or `plugins/SubAuth/config.yml`, make sure you delete the placeholder tokens.
5. Restart your server, and SubAuth will be enabled!

For your subscribers to join, they just have to link their twitch account on the `subauth.thatgamerblue.com` server, then they will be automatically able to join the server!  
If you wish for subscribers to multiple users to be able to join the server, simply add the other creators tokens into the config file, and if a user is subscriber to any of them, they will be able to join.

## Issues

Please report any issues on [the GitHub page](https://github.com/ThatGamerBlue/subauth)
